# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] bd close <id1> <id2> ...   (close completed issues)
```



## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: stealth mode (no git ops)
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

## Persistent Memories (28)

Stored via `bd remember`. Update in place with `bd remember --key <key> "new content"`. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`. Remove with `bd forget <key>`.

### autosession-bead-mutation-schema-auto-session-coordinator-us
autosession-bead-mutation-schema: Auto-session coordinator uses metadata for cardinality-1 classifications (agent=human|claude|shared set on first claim, autosession_slug=<slug> rewritten each claim — last-toucher semantics, queryable via bd list --metadata-field) and labels only for transient operational state (autosession-deferred, autosession-blocked — added on defer/block, removed on next claim). No accumulating src:autosession-<slug> labels — the full attribution history lives in notes with the required prefix '[autosession/<slug> <ISO-8601>]', filterable via bd list --notes-contains. Rationale: metadata enforces cardinality invariants; labels accumulate and become noise across multi-session re-touches; notes carry transition rationale inline. Aligned 2026-04-21.

### autosession-memory-format-bd-remember-entries-added-by
autosession-memory-format: bd remember entries added by the autosession coordinator must start with 'autosession[<slug>]:' and end with a pointer to the session's exit-summary.md for audit provenance. Example format: 'autosession[tooling-wave-1-3-20260421]: <fact> (see tasks/sessions/tooling-wave-1-3-20260421/exit-summary.md#discoveries)'. The prefix lets 'bd memories autosession' surface the cohort and 'bd memories autosession[<slug>]' narrow to one session. The pointer lets /import-session reviewers drill back to context without guessing. Gated by lifecycle.memory.can_remember in the beads template.

### autosession-preship-bead-topology
Topology lesson from 2026-04-22 auto-session pre-ship bead creation. (1) bd mol/bond is for reusable WORKFLOW TEMPLATES (protos/molecules), NOT for general-purpose epic grouping or taxonomic nesting — use parent-child for hierarchical containment, bond only when composing template workflows. (2) bd enforces a directional constraint: tasks cannot block epics ('epics can only block other epics, not tasks'). Workaround: create a dedicated release-gate task as a child of the epic; route hard 'blocks' edges from external tasks through the release-gate task. Epic-completion semantics then handle the transitive gate. (3) 'discovered-from' edges should trace the actual causal chain — the investigation where a fact was uncovered — not the consumer bead that acts on the fact. (4) For aggregates of one-off work with independent children, a plain epic beats molecule primitives; bd mol distill can promote an ad-hoc epic into a proto LATER if a reusable pattern emerges. (5) Closed-at-creation provenance beads (filing completed research as a task for downstream discovered-from anchors) are a valid and useful pattern — the bead's purpose is audit-trail, not workflow.

### autosession-status-transition-mechanics-bd-1-0-2
autosession-status-transition-mechanics: bd 1.0.2 has no per-transition metadata for status changes other than close (bd close --reason). Non-close transitions use 'bd update --status <new> --append-notes "[autosession/<slug> <ISO-8601>] <from> -> <new>; <rationale>"' in one invocation — notes accumulate in the single notes field, are timestamped by the coordinator-provided prefix, and are filterable via --notes-contains. Respect user's custom statuses (bd config set status.custom review,testing): coordinator default on-finish = review, not closed.

### bd-formula-not-for-runtime-policy-bd-formulas
bd-formula-not-for-runtime-policy: bd formulas describe issue-DAG spawn templates (variables, steps, parent/child edges) cooked into protos and poured into real beads. They do NOT constrain runtime coordinator behavior on existing beads — allowed status transitions, note prefixes, label can/can't-touch, metadata rewrite timing all live outside the formula layer. When a policy is ~80% runtime behavior and ~20% create-time, ship YAML-read-by-coordinator and skip the formula conversion; partial conversion splits the policy across two mental models without payoff. Re-evaluate if bd grows runtime-policy primitives (e.g., a bd gate shape blocking out-of-allowlist transitions). Verified 2026-04-22 via J121-7bv.1 probe: bd formula list ships zero bundled examples, schema is documented only via --help, formulas failed validation on both flat and wrapped JSON attempts.

### bd-jira-sync-pull-incremental-timing-gap-sync
bd jira sync --pull incremental timing gap: sync appends AND updated >= <last-sync-timestamp> to JQL, so tickets not updated since the last sync run are silently skipped even if they match the base JQL filter. Root cause of BOCO-18077 missing on initial session sync (2026-04-21). Fix: run 'bd jira sync --pull' again after any period of inactivity to catch stale-but-active tickets; there is no --full-rescan flag. Coverage check: compare 'bd list --label=src:jira | wc -l' against Jira API total. Also: 3 tickets (BOCO-16303, BOCO-17538, BOCO-18128) permanently absent because they were Done before the first full sync ran — not worth importing.

### bd-list-json-trailing-footer
bd list --json appends a human-readable 'Showing N issues...' footer AFTER the JSON array when results are truncated by --limit (default 50). This breaks strict JSON parsers. Workaround: pipe through `sed -n '1,/^]$/p'` to cut at the closing bracket line. bd ready --json does NOT have this bug. Affects: /show slash command (J121-9kp.2.15), any future tooling that consumes bd list --json output.

### beads-reconcile-design-j121-1b1-design-doc-for
beads-reconcile-design-J121-1b1: Design doc for label-inheritance architecture lives in J121-1b1's description + design fields (bd show J121-1b1). Hybrid C+D+E: (C) bd-timew wrapper forward-propagates inheritable labels to new children, (D) beads-reconcile script walks parent-child edges and materializes missing inheritable dimensions, (E) epics declare the canonical tuple once for org transparency. E alone doesn't work — bd --label is exact-match, doesn't walk graph. See also: billing-labels-on-jira-beads-all-src-jira for the 2026-04-21 manual pass that established the pattern. Resume the design conversation by reading bd show J121-1b1's design field — 7 open questions are captured there.

### beads-viewer-license-risk-accepted
Decision 2026-04-20 (mhaynes): bv (beads_viewer, github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer) upstream LICENSE is MIT-with-rider that explicitly denies rights to OpenAI, Anthropic, their Affiliates, and 'any person or entity acting directly or indirectly on behalf of' them — including 'copying, modifying, merging, ..., executing, benchmarking, testing, analyzing, indexing, or incorporating the Software into any ... evaluation harness, or pipeline for machine learning or other automated systems.' Claude Code invoking 'bv --robot-*' on behalf of the user plausibly falls under the 'acting on behalf of' clause, and --robot-* flags are designed for AI consumption, making the 'pipeline for automated systems' clause directly on-point. User verified (with Claude assist) that beads_viewer is NOT listed in github.com/jeremylongshore/claude-code-plugins-plus-skills despite author having OTHER unrelated plugins there — marketplace aggregation would not override upstream license anyway. Risk accepted for personal-tooling use (scope:local, client:personal, case:area:claude) based on: (a) user is BOCO employee not an Anthropic affiliate; (b) 'acting on behalf of' clause's enforceability against end-users of a Claude Code client is genuinely ambiguous; (c) personal-tooling use is not commercial redistribution. Risk NOT cleared for: (a) team-wide or BOCO-facing adoption; (b) incorporation into billable deliverables; (c) public redistribution of derivative tooling. Integration surfaces that invoke --robot-*: /next (delegated to --robot-next via J121 bead-for-refactor), /triage (planned, new bead J121-tbd), /session-checkpoint enrichment (planned). If license stance changes or upstream rider softens, revisit integrations.

### billing-labels-on-jira-beads-all-src-jira
billing-labels-on-jira-beads: All src:jira beads and their descendants carry svc:'Technology Services' + case:'07_04 Portal Stabilization Phase 1.1 (Project Task)' (bd labels accept spaces/parens). Applied 2026-04-21 to closure of 49 beads (27 open + 11 closed seeds + 11 descendants). New Jira-synced beads and their descendants should inherit these labels; bd doesn't propagate automatically.

### boco-18077-j121-n39-open-items-2026-04
BOCO-18077 / J121-n39 open items (2026-04-21): (1) Review worker pipeline YAMLs for variable group structure and KeyVault refs per DEC-013 in J121-pipelines/docs/DECISIONS.md — still outstanding. (2) ADO notification config for Kristi/Taylor/Joanna on prod deploys — not started, no bead. (3) TRUNCATE + CREATE TABLE grants codified in Terraform for DEV/TEST — PROD done. (4) J121-obv (DB name + Jinja for STAGING procs) and J121-6da (VISIT_CYCLE table approach) both need Yan Sheng confirmation before repo migrations can be updated. Full status in J121-n39 notes.

### bv-extended-robot-flags-beyond-robot-triage-used
bv extended robot flags (beyond --robot-triage used in /next): --robot-next (single top pick + claim command), --robot-plan (parallel execution tracks with unblocks lists), --robot-insights (full graph metrics: PageRank, betweenness, HITS, eigenvector, k-core, critical path), --robot-alerts (stale issues, blocking cascades, priority mismatches), --robot-suggest (hygiene: duplicate detection, missing deps, label suggestions, cycle breaks), --robot-diff --diff-since <ref> (changes since a git ref: new/closed/modified), --robot-graph --graph-format json|dot|mermaid (dependency graph export)

### bv-output-modifiers-and-scope-flags-format-toon
bv output modifiers and scope flags: --format toon (token-optimized output, lower LLM context usage — works with any --robot-* flag), --recipe actionable (pre-filter: ready-to-work beads only, no blockers), --recipe high-impact (pre-filter: top PageRank scores), --as-of HEAD~N (historical point-in-time analysis, works with any --robot-* flag, e.g. bv --robot-insights --as-of HEAD~30), --label <value> (scope analysis to label's subgraph)

### chezmoi-feature-development-happens-in-the-linked-worktree
Chezmoi feature development happens in the linked worktree at ~/Source/chezmoi-features (created via 'git worktree add' from ~/.local/share/chezmoi). Canonical clone stays on main so chezmoi CLI/daemon stay in sync; feature branches live in the worktree. One branch per worktree is a hard git constraint, so switching features means 'cd ~/Source/chezmoi-features && git switch <branch>' — don't create additional worktrees unless truly parallel development is needed. See project CLAUDE.md 'Session Discipline' section for the full rule.

### claude-config-repo-separate-initiative
Decision 2026-04-20: the shareable Claude config repo is a SEPARATE top-level initiative (J121-014), not a wave inside J121-9kp. Rationale: J121-9kp produces the payload (skills, commands, agents, scripts); the config repo packages and distributes them. Conflating the two would force install-script maintenance against a moving target. Implementation must not start until J121-9kp Waves 1-3 are substantially complete, and when it does start it must export all planning context/artifacts to the new repo/directory (not J121's auto-memory or tasks/).

### claude-settings-location-editormode
Decision 2026-04-20 (confirmed): editorMode=vim must live in ~/.claude.json, NOT ~/.claude/settings.json. User tested settings.json placement and Claude Code ignored it. Fix deployed via chezmoi 'modify' script at ~/.local/share/chezmoi/home/modify_private_dot_claude.json — jq pipeline that surgically sets .editorMode without chezmoi owning the full file. Prefix order matters: modify_private_ (not private_modify_) — with private_ first, chezmoi consumes only the private_ attribute and treats 'modify_dot_claude.json' as a literal filename. Script is defensive: passes stdin through unchanged if jq missing; exits 0 on empty stdin (target doesn't exist) to avoid stubbing a file Claude Code might overwrite without merging. Executable bit not needed on source (chezmoi copies contents to temp file and sets bit). private_ attribute preserves the 0600 mode on ~/.claude.json (holds OAuth tokens). Claude Code picks up editorMode changes live — no restart required (confirmed 2026-04-20). Rejected full-file chezmoi-management: 47KB of 64KB churn-heavy state (projects, counters, timestamps) that would conflict across machines and leak oauthAccount in a git remote.

### dicklesworthstone-review-skip-decisions
Decision 2026-04-21 (mhaynes): SKIP adoption of 13 Dicklesworthstone projects per multi-agent review verdicts (J121-6ka, closed). Tools + primary skip rationale: (1) repo_updater — redundant with existing pre-push hooks; (2) tsap_mcp_server — overbuild for current scale, token-cost concern; (3) agent_settings_backup_script — Chezmoi already handles this cleaner; (4) coding_agent_usage_tracker — redundant with bd-timew; (5) claude_code_agent_farm — assumes multi-agent parallel workflow user doesn't run; (6) rust_scriptbots — experimental/toy, no production use case; (7) ultimate_mcp_server — massive token injection, overbuild; (8) ultimate_mcp_client — alternate Claude client, redundant with Claude Code; (9) system_resource_protection_script — not solving a stated pain point; (10) source_to_prompt_tui — claude-mem + smart_outline MCP covers this; (11) agentic_coding_flywheel_setup — opinionated scaffolding that conflicts with existing stack; (12) markdown_web_browser — niche; (13) rano is ADOPT not skip (user override — see rano-adoption-counter-rationale memory). FULL REVIEWS: tasks/dicklesworthstone-review/deliverables/dicklesworthstone_projects.md. Re-evaluation trigger: if any of (a) multi-agent workflow is adopted, (b) Bold Orange standardizes on AI tooling, (c) existing stack loses coverage in one of these areas.

### estimate-label-convention
Estimate label convention: three dimensions — est-h:N (hours, only dimension bd-timew variance reports consult), est-p:N (story points, scrum), est-t:S (t-shirt size: XS/S/M/L/XL, scrum). Issues may carry any subset. Apply at creation time; retrofit only when touching issues for other reasons. Hours use whole/half integers (est-h:0.5 min). Query via bd list --label-pattern='est-*:*' or --label-pattern='est-h:*'. Full convention + rationale in auto-memory feedback-time-tracking.md. Decided 2026-04-19.

### rano-adoption-counter-rationale
Decision 2026-04-21 (mhaynes): install rano (github.com/Dicklesworthstone/rano) as BOCO AI governance / telemetry PoC, OVERRIDING the dicklesworthstone-review agent's 'skip' verdict. Counter-rationale: (a) negligible processing cost (/proc polling, sub-ms overhead), (b) manageable storage (SQLite, rotate-able), (c) zero token cost (out-of-process, no MCP injection), (d) BOCO is actively seeking AI governance tooling — Erick already standardized New Relic telemetry for devs (see ~/.zshrc env vars) — so pre-installing rano positions it as a potential reporting/auditing data source for BOCO AI policy. Current scope: personal PoC, not team-wide; success metric = 'useful data accumulates' rather than 'immediate problem solved'. License rider applies (same OpenAI/Anthropic Restricted Parties clause as bv); risk accepted for personal-tooling scope under same reasoning as bd memories beads-viewer-license-risk-accepted. If PoC yields actionable patterns, next step is proposing to Erick as part of BOCO's AI governance posture. Implementation: Chezmoi installer, systemd user unit for daemon, weekly email report via systemd timer. Full review: tasks/dicklesworthstone-review/deliverables/dicklesworthstone_projects.md (rano section).

### show-vs-next-split
Decision 2026-04-20: /show and /next are separate slash commands, not one command with modes. /show = read-only filter+list over bd list/bd ready with ergonomic flag names (--task/--epic/--area/--service/--assignee/--status); /next = scoring/recommendation layered on top of bd ready. Rationale: single responsibility, /show answers 'what exists that matches X?', /next answers 'what should I work on next?' — mixing them forces every /show invocation to load scoring heuristics it doesn't need. Beads: J121-9kp.2.15 (/show), J121-9kp.2.13 (/next). Alternative considered: merge into /next with --filter mode flag — rejected as premature abstraction.

### slash-command-frontmatter-conventions-apply-whenever-editing
Slash command frontmatter conventions (apply whenever editing any ~/.claude/commands/*.md or project .claude/commands/*.md file): (1) argument-hint field must accurately reflect all accepted flags after every edit — update it whenever flags are added, removed, or renamed; (2) timestamps array is append-only: add a new entry {action: updated, at: <ISO-8601>, actor: <git user.name>, note: <one-line summary of change>} for every edit; (3) comments array: add a new bullet for substantive changes (new flag families, behavioral shifts); mechanical fixes go in timestamps[].note only — do not duplicate in comments. These conventions are enforced by /edit-command step 7 invariants.

### smartstore-themes-working-branch-is-origin-prototype-not
SmartStore_Themes working branch is origin/Prototype not origin/master. Local master tracks Prototype (0 ahead/behind). origin/master is stale/unused — ignore 103-ahead sweep warning. All theme work flows through Prototype branch.

### svc-labels-unused-no-beads-currently-have-svc
svc-labels-unused: No beads currently have svc: labels even though the label taxonomy supports them and bd-timew.yaml maps area: labels to billing tuples. User flagged this 2026-04-21 as a gap to fix. When adding svc: labels, the value should match the Svc field from bd-timew start output (e.g., svc:Technology-Services). Start by adding to all billable beads under BOCO-18077 and BOCO-17078.

### terraform-drift-diagnostic-procedure
When a terraform plan shows unexpected drift (force-replace, mysterious in-place updates), triangulate from three sources: (1) git log -G '<attribute>' -- <tf-file> to find when the attribute was added + git blame for the author/rationale; (2) Snowflake SNOWFLAKE.ACCOUNT_USAGE.QUERY_HISTORY with query_text ilike filter on the affected DDL (365-day retention, shows terraform_provider_usage_tracking comments identifying TF-driven vs manual); (3) Azure blob last-modified on the tfstate file (az storage blob list --account-name journeyblobstorage --container-name tfstate) cross-referenced against the fix commit's timestamp. Hypothesis: attribute added to code, applied to some envs but not others. Example: J121-n39.4 2026-04-22, DEV found stuck pre-commit-0ea822d while TEST+PROD were applied post-commit. Blob versioning is NOT enabled on journeyblobstorage as of 2026-04-22; only last-modified is available — no per-apply history until J121-h1x is resolved.

### terraform-proc-owner-grants-truncate-combined-with-dml
terraform-proc-owner-grants-truncate-combined-with-dml: In terraform/modules/snowflake/grants.tf, TRUNCATE is combined with DML (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) in the proc_owner_mdm_tables and proc_owner_mdm_future_tables resources rather than as a separate grant block. Rationale: every proc that truncates an MDM table also does DML on it, so splitting would inflate the state graph without clarifying intent. Diverges from the original J121-n39.1 plan which specified three blocks (create_table + truncate + dml). If that invariant ever breaks (a proc truncates a table it has no DML on), split TRUNCATE back into its own resource.

### time-tracking-option-a-decision
Decision 2026-04-18: time-tracking billing-tuple storage = Option A (sidecar config .beads/bd-timew.yaml with pattern-based label mappings), NOT Option B (merged |-delimited labels on every issue). Why: per-issue tuple duplication (~95% of issues share the same tuple); NetSuite case strings contain spaces/punctuation that break Beads dimension:value label grammar and are Jira-sync-unsafe; case renames would force bulk relabeling; merged labels defeat the Option B prefix-namespaced label taxonomy. Wrapper: bd start <id> reads issue labels, resolves against sidecar, runs timew start with client:/case:/svc: tags. Convention details in auto-memory feedback-time-tracking.md. J121 sidecar at .beads/bd-timew.yaml (patterns list currently a scaffold - full NetSuite case list still needed).

### title-convention-for-src-jira-beads-always-prepend
Title convention for src:jira beads: always prepend the Jira key as 'BOCO-XXXXX · <original title>'. Example: 'BOCO-18077 · Great Clips | Pipeline Review/Validation'. Apply when creating via bd create or when a jira sync imports a new ticket. Rationale: bd list and bd show surface the Jira key immediately without needing to inspect external_ref. Applied retroactively to all src:jira beads on 2026-04-21.

### tutor-command-scope
Decision 2026-04-20: /tutor slash command is scoped to the shareable Claude config repo initiative (J121-014), NOT J121-9kp Wave 2. Rationale: in isolation /tutor overlaps with the planned /docs command (J121-9kp.2.4); bundled with the shareable repo it earns its keep as discoverability for users who didn't build the tools themselves. The --provenance flag forces a useful discipline: per-tool source:/decided: frontmatter that skills and commands should probably carry anyway. Expected flags: --short/--long, --provenance, --history, --example[s].

S296 Redo Build System Tutorial — conceptual overview, use-cases, API design, worked examples, and minimal POSIX sh implementation (Apr 22, 8:05 PM)
**Investigated**: The redo build system as conceived by D.J. Bernstein and implemented by Avery Pennarun (apenwarr/redo): its dependency model, change-detection mechanism, .do file conventions, core commands, and comparison to Make/CMake/Bazel

**Learned**: Redo inverts Make's dependency model: instead of a central Makefile declaring dependencies, individual .do shell scripts call redo-ifchange at runtime to declare and satisfy their own dependencies. This makes the dependency graph emergent from actual builds rather than a maintained manifest.

    Redo uses content hashing (not mtime) for change detection — touching a file without changing it does nothing; reverting a file correctly triggers rebuilds. Build output is written atomically via a temp file ($3) moved to the target only on success.

    The .do file lookup order is: exact match (foo.bar.do), extension wildcard (default.bar.do), catch-all (default.do). Scripts receive $1=target, $2=base (stem without final extension), $3=temp output path.

    Core commands: redo (build targets), redo-ifchange (declare deps + ensure up to date), redo-always (mark as always-rebuild/phony), redo-ifcreated (rebuild if absent file appears).

    Key advantages over Make: no stale dependency declarations possible (deps declared at execution time), correct content-hash semantics, atomic output, shell-native build scripts. Key disadvantages: minimal ecosystem, POSIX-only, no IDE integration, no parallel build support in minimal implementations.

**Completed**: Delivered a complete tutorial covering:
    1. Conceptual overview of the runtime dependency declaration model
    2. Use-case analysis and comparison table vs Make/CMake/Bazel/Redo
    3. Full API reference (.do conventions, $1/$2/$3 args, all four commands, env vars)
    4. Five worked examples: single C file, C with automatic header deps, static site generator, protobuf codegen chain, phony targets
    5. A complete minimal redo implementation in ~150 lines of POSIX sh, dispatched by $0 symlink pattern, with content hashing, staleness checking, atomic output, and all four commands implemented
    6. Explicit list of what the minimal implementation omits vs production (parallel builds, cycle detection, out-of-tree builds, stdout capture)

**Next Steps**: No follow-on work indicated — this was a self-contained educational request with a complete response delivered.

message.content:
Provided the conversation transcript is up to date, I'd like to break from this topic for other work for awhile.  Before we move on, do the following:
- Ensure the transcript and verbatim JSONL record of this conversation is up to date through the previous turn, then stop spooling (i.e., stop appending to both the transcript and the JSONL exact session record after confirming they're complete through the last turn).
- Create a reusable command for "spooling" a conversation transcript to a file + JSONL conversation record like we did this session, using the script you produced earlier to map the JSONL records to text appended to the transcript.  The command should be called /start-transcript, and should be Chezmoi-managed.  It should take a single mandatory argument specifying where the transcript should be written.  Optional arguments should include --full, specifying that the transcript should be an **exact** replay of the conversation including all tool-use requests, output, etc. from Claude turns; --conversation, indicating that the fidelity should be exact only for the user's prompts and the "main" Claude response turns, should exclude all intermediate Claude turns produced while thinking (e.g., tool-use requests and output, turns like "Now I see the full context" output prior to the full response, etc.), and should be formatted as Markdown to allow easily following the conversation thread; and --context-only, indicating that the transcript should be built from only what's available in Claude's current context and should not attempt high-fidelity reconstruction using the JSONL record.
- Create companion commands /pause-transcript, /resume-transcript, and /stop-transcript, with the obvious functionality.  (/pause-transcript and /stop-transcript can probably just be aliases.)  Alternatively, if you think a single /transcript command with flags for starting, stopping, and pausing plus the flags specified in the previous bullet would be a better shape, implement that pattern instead.  If you have a different suggestion, let me know and we'll discuss.

content.text: Let me start by finalizing the current spool, then design the commands.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: jira-show
3	description: Fetch a Jira ticket + recent comments and present a context briefing with local-mirror pointers
4	argument-hint: <JIRA-KEY>
5	author: Michael Haynes
6	scope: global
7	tags: [jira, mcp, atlassian, read-only]
8	timestamps:
9	  - action: created
10	    at: 2026-04-20T03:52:31-05:00
11	    actor: Michael Haynes
12	comments:
13	  - "Source: J121-9kp.2.2 Jira workflow bundle (2026-04-20). Paired with /jira-create."
14	  - "Motivation: picking up inherited, stale, or handoff work needed a one-shot brief that went beyond 'bd show' — status, assignee, priority, description, recent comments, and pointers into the local tasks/<KEY>/ mirror."
15	  - "Projected use: invoke when returning to unfamiliar work, responding to a Jira mention, or before posting an update. Read-only; never mutates Jira. Comment bodies arrive as ADF despite responseContentFormat=markdown (known MCP quirk)."
16	related: [/jira-create, /draft-comment, /post-comment, "jira-conventions skill"]
17	---
18	
19	# /jira-show — Context briefing for an existing Jira ticket
20	
21	Fetch a Jira ticket and its recent comments, then present a briefing — status, assignee, priority, description, recent activity, local mirror pointers. Useful when picking up inherited or stale work.
22	
23	Argument: $ARGUMENTS
24	Single token: the Jira key (e.g. `BOCO-18077`). Additional tokens are ignored.
25	
26	## Instructions
27	
28	1. **Require an argument.** If `$ARGUMENTS` is empty:
29	
30	   ```
31	   /jira-show: missing ticket id
32	   usage: /jira-show <JIRA-KEY>
33	     e.g. /jira-show BOCO-18077
34	   ```
35	
36	   and stop.
37	
38	2. **Validate the key.** Match `^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]+-\d+$`. If it doesn't match, refuse — do not attempt a fetch or guess from partial input.
39	
40	3. **Fetch the issue.** Call `mcp__claude_ai_Atlassian__getJiraIssue` with:
41	
42	   - `cloudId`: `80b04637-628f-4df2-8bfa-012de201c08c`
43	   - `issueIdOrKey`: the key
44	   - `responseContentFormat`: `"markdown"` — the **description** will flatten to markdown; **comment bodies may still arrive as ADF** and must be handled per step 7. ADO URLs are fine at read time (the Smart-Link upgrade problem only matters on write).
45	   - `fields`: `["summary","status","priority","issuetype","assignee","reporter","created","updated","labels","description","comment"]`
46	
47	   If the call returns a 404 or similar, surface the error verbatim and stop.
48	
49	4. **Ensure comments are loaded.** If the `comment` field returned truncated or empty but the issue clearly has comments, fetch the last 10 via `mcp__claude_ai_Atlassian__fetch` against `/rest/api/3/issue/<key>/comment?maxResults=10&orderBy=-created`. Take the most recent 5 chronologically (oldest→newest within the window).
50	
51	5. **Check the local mirror.** In parallel:
52	   - Run `bd list --label=src:jira --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.external_ref | endswith("/<KEY>")) | .id'` to find a mirrored bead ID. (If no mirror, note "no mirror" and move on — do not create one.) **`bd` must run from the repo root**, i.e. the parent of `.beads/`, not inside `.beads/` itself. If the user isn't in a beads workspace, skip this subcheck silently.
53	   - Check `tasks/<KEY>/` existence. If it exists, list any `comments/*.md` files (draft Jira comments, posted or otherwise).
54	
55	6. **Compose the briefing.** Use this structure verbatim:
56	
57	   ```
58	   ── <KEY> ─ <summary>
59	
60	   Status:    <name>
61	   Type:      <issueType>
62	   Priority:  <name>
63	   Assignee:  <displayName or "Unassigned">
64	   Reporter:  <displayName>
65	   Created:   <YYYY-MM-DD>
66	   Updated:   <YYYY-MM-DD>
67	   Labels:    <comma-separated or "(none)">
68	
69	   ── Description ──
70	   <description body, flattened to markdown>
71	
72	   ── Recent comments (<n> of <total>) ──
73	   <author> · <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM>
74	   <body>
75	
76	   ────────
77	
78	   <next comment…>
79	
80	   ── Local links ──
81	   Beads:      <bead-id> | (no mirror)
82	   Task dir:   tasks/<KEY>/ | (none)
83	   Drafts:     comments/2026-04-20-analysis.md [posted 2026-04-20] | (none)
84	   ```
85	
86	7. **Rendering.** With `responseContentFormat: "markdown"` the description pre-flattens but comment bodies typically still arrive as ADF. For ADF content, flatten to markdown covering: headings, paragraphs, bullet/numbered lists (preserving nesting), code blocks, inline `code` / **bold** / _italic_, links (`[text](url)`), mentions (display `@<Display Name>` if `text` attr is set, else `@<accountId>`). Render unknown node types as `[unsupported: <type>]` rather than dropping them. For markdown bodies, **strip over-escaped wiki artifacts** (`\*`, `\[`, `\]`, `\~`, `\_`, `\#`) except inside code fences — the server's markdown flattener escapes them defensively and they hurt readability.
87	
88	8. **Do not editorialize.** This is a briefing, not an analysis. No "next steps", no interpretation. Present the facts.
89	
90	## Invariants
91	
92	- **Read-only.** Never transition, comment, edit, or modify the Jira ticket. Never create the Beads mirror if one is missing.
93	- **Never guess** values that aren't in the API response. If a field is missing, say so (`Priority: (unset)`) rather than inventing.
94	- **Never truncate** the description. Summaries belong in the task/commit/PR flow, not in a briefing.
95	
96	## Related
97	
98	- `/jira-create <summary>` — the companion that creates a new ticket.
99	- `/draft-comment <key> [guidance]` — draft a follow-up comment after reviewing the briefing.
100	- `/post-comment <draft-path>` — post a drafted comment.
101	- `jira-conventions` skill — when drafting a reply, these rules apply.
102

file.content:
---
name: jira-show
description: Fetch a Jira ticket + recent comments and present a context briefing with local-mirror pointers
argument-hint: <JIRA-KEY>
author: Michael Haynes
scope: global
tags: [jira, mcp, atlassian, read-only]
timestamps:
  - action: created
    at: 2026-04-20T03:52:31-05:00
    actor: Michael Haynes
comments:
  - "Source: J121-9kp.2.2 Jira workflow bundle (2026-04-20). Paired with /jira-create."
  - "Motivation: picking up inherited, stale, or handoff work needed a one-shot brief that went beyond 'bd show' — status, assignee, priority, description, recent comments, and pointers into the local tasks/<KEY>/ mirror."
  - "Projected use: invoke when returning to unfamiliar work, responding to a Jira mention, or before posting an update. Read-only; never mutates Jira. Comment bodies arrive as ADF despite responseContentFormat=markdown (known MCP quirk)."
related: [/jira-create, /draft-comment, /post-comment, "jira-conventions skill"]
---

# /jira-show — Context briefing for an existing Jira ticket

Fetch a Jira ticket and its recent comments, then present a briefing — status, assignee, priority, description, recent activity, local mirror pointers. Useful when picking up inherited or stale work.

Argument: $ARGUMENTS
Single token: the Jira key (e.g. `BOCO-18077`). Additional tokens are ignored.

## Instructions

1. **Require an argument.** If `$ARGUMENTS` is empty:

   ```
   /jira-show: missing ticket id
   usage: /jira-show <JIRA-KEY>
     e.g. /jira-show BOCO-18077
   ```

   and stop.

2. **Validate the key.** Match `^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]+-\d+$`. If it doesn't match, refuse — do not attempt a fetch or guess from partial input.

3. **Fetch the issue.** Call `mcp__claude_ai_Atlassian__getJiraIssue` with:

   - `cloudId`: `80b04637-628f-4df2-8bfa-012de201c08c`
   - `issueIdOrKey`: the key
   - `responseContentFormat`: `"markdown"` — the **description** will flatten to markdown; **comment bodies may still arrive as ADF** and must be handled per step 7. ADO URLs are fine at read time (the Smart-Link upgrade problem only matters on write).
   - `fields`: `["summary","status","priority","issuetype","assignee","reporter","created","updated","labels","description","comment"]`

   If the call returns a 404 or similar, surface the error verbatim and stop.

4. **Ensure comments are loaded.** If the `comment` field returned truncated or empty but the issue clearly has comments, fetch the last 10 via `mcp__claude_ai_Atlassian__fetch` against `/rest/api/3/issue/<key>/comment?maxResults=10&orderBy=-created`. Take the most recent 5 chronologically (oldest→newest within the window).

5. **Check the local mirror.** In parallel:
   - Run `bd list --label=src:jira --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.external_ref | endswith("/<KEY>")) | .id'` to find a mirrored bead ID. (If no mirror, note "no mirror" and move on — do not create one.) **`bd` must run from the repo root**, i.e. the parent of `.beads/`, not inside `.beads/` itself. If the user isn't in a beads workspace, skip this subcheck silently.
   - Check `tasks/<KEY>/` existence. If it exists, list any `comments/*.md` files (draft Jira comments, posted or otherwise).

6. **Compose the briefing.** Use this structure verbatim:

   ```
   ── <KEY> ─ <summary>

   Status:    <name>
   Type:      <issueType>
   Priority:  <name>
   Assignee:  <displayName or "Unassigned">
   Reporter:  <displayName>
   Created:   <YYYY-MM-DD>
   Updated:   <YYYY-MM-DD>
   Labels:    <comma-separated or "(none)">

   ── Description ──
   <description body, flattened to markdown>

   ── Recent comments (<n> of <total>) ──
   <author> · <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM>
   <body>

   ────────

   <next comment…>

   ── Local links ──
   Beads:      <bead-id> | (no mirror)
   Task dir:   tasks/<KEY>/ | (none)
   Drafts:     comments/2026-04-20-analysis.md [posted 2026-04-20] | (none)
   ```

7. **Rendering.** With `responseContentFormat: "markdown"` the description pre-flattens but comment bodies typically still arrive as ADF. For ADF content, flatten to markdown covering: headings, paragraphs, bullet/numbered lists (preserving nesting), code blocks, inline `code` / **bold** / _italic_, links (`[text](url)`), mentions (display `@<Display Name>` if `text` attr is set, else `@<accountId>`). Render unknown node types as `[unsupported: <type>]` rather than dropping them. For markdown bodies, **strip over-escaped wiki artifacts** (`\*`, `\[`, `\]`, `\~`, `\_`, `\#`) except inside code fences — the server's markdown flattener escapes them defensively and they hurt readability.

8. **Do not editorialize.** This is a briefing, not an analysis. No "next steps", no interpretation. Present the facts.

## Invariants

- **Read-only.** Never transition, comment, edit, or modify the Jira ticket. Never create the Beads mirror if one is missing.
- **Never guess** values that aren't in the API response. If a field is missing, say so (`Priority: (unset)`) rather than inventing.
- **Never truncate** the description. Summaries belong in the task/commit/PR flow, not in a briefing.

## Related

- `/jira-create <summary>` — the companion that creates a new ticket.
- `/draft-comment <key> [guidance]` — draft a follow-up comment after reviewing the briefing.
- `/post-comment <draft-path>` — post a drafted comment.
- `jira-conventions` skill — when drafting a reply, these rules apply.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: start
3	description: Begin work on a Beads issue — claim it and start a Timewarrior interval tagged with its billing tuple
4	argument-hint: "<bead-id> | --jira <JIRA-KEY>"
5	author: Michael Haynes
6	scope: global
7	tags: [time-tracking, beads, timewarrior]
8	timestamps:
9	  - action: created
10	    at: 2026-04-20T00:35:06-05:00
11	    actor: Michael Haynes
12	  - action: updated
13	    at: 2026-04-21T11:30:00-05:00
14	    actor: Michael Haynes
15	    note: "Added --jira <KEY> as alternative to positional bead ID, with Jira-fallback resolution procedure"
16	comments:
17	  - "Source: J121-9kp.1 Wave 1 time-tracking bundle (2026-04-20). Companion to /stop, /switch, /status and the bd-timew bridge script at ~/.local/bin/bd-timew."
18	  - "Motivation: claiming a bead and starting time tracking were two separate rituals (bd update --claim + timew start) that were easy to skip. One command enforces the pairing."
19	  - "Projected use: invoke when starting work on a specific bead. Epic-level /start is OK for triage/refinement; /switch to a child bead as soon as concrete work begins so billing tuple resolves correctly."
20	  - "2026-04-21: Added --jira <JIRA-KEY> flag as an alternative to the positional bead ID. Resolves via local title search → Jira MCP verify → bd jira sync → retry; surfaces known pitfalls and offers manual-mirror fallback on continued failure. Positional ID and --jira are mutually exclusive."
21	related: [/stop, /switch, /status, /time-report]
22	---
23	
24	# /start — Begin work on a Beads issue
25	
26	Claim a Beads issue and start a Timewarrior interval tagged with its resolved billing tuple. Thin wrapper over `bd-timew start`.
27	
28	Arguments: $ARGUMENTS
29	
30	## Instructions
31	
32	1. **Parse and validate arguments.** If `$ARGUMENTS` is empty or whitespace, print:
33	   ```
34	   /start: missing bead id
35	   usage: /start <bead-id>        (e.g. /start J121-abc)
36	          /start --jira <KEY>     (e.g. /start --jira BOCO-18077)
37	   ```
38	   and stop. Do **not** infer a bead from `bd ready`, in-progress lists, or context. Starting the timer on the wrong bead corrupts billing.
39	
40	   Recognized forms:
41	   - Positional bead ID: first token that does not start with `-`
42	   - `--jira <KEY>`: flag + value (mutually exclusive with positional ID)
43	   - Both present simultaneously → usage error and stop.
44	
45	2. **Resolve the bead ID.**
46	   - If a positional bead ID was given → `resolved_id = <id>`. Skip to step 3.
47	   - If `--jira <KEY>` → run the **Jira key resolution** procedure:
48	
49	     **A. Local lookup.**
50	     ```bash
51	     bd list --label=src:jira --json 2>/dev/null | sed -n '1,/^]$/p' | \
52	       jq -r --arg k "KEY" '.[] | select(.title | test($k; "i")) | .id' | head -1
53	     ```
54	     If a bead ID is returned → `resolved_id = <that id>`. Proceed to step 3.
55	
56	     **B. Jira verification** (if local lookup returned nothing). Call the Atlassian MCP tool `getJiraIssue` (cloudId `80b04637-628f-4df2-8bfa-012de201c08c`, issueIdOrKey `<KEY>`).
57	     - If MCP returns an error or 404: print `/start: ticket <KEY> not found in Jira or no access. Verify the key and Jira authentication.` and stop.
58	     - If ticket found → proceed to C.
59	
60	     **C. Sync and retry.** Run `bd jira sync --pull` via Bash. Re-run the local lookup from A. If found → `resolved_id = <that id>`. Proceed to step 3.
61	
62	     **D. Diagnose and surface** (if still not found after sync). Run `bd config show | grep pull_jql` to get the configured JQL. Check whether the Jira ticket's summary and assignee match it. Then report:
63	     - "Ticket `<KEY>` exists in Jira but could not be imported into Beads."
64	     - List applicable known reasons:
65	       * *JQL mismatch*: ticket isn't assigned to `currentUser()` or summary doesn't satisfy the filter — sync will never auto-import it. See CLAUDE.md pitfall #26.
66	       * *Incremental timing gap*: ticket matches the JQL but hasn't been updated since the last sync run. Known pitfall: `bd-jira-sync-pull-incremental-timing-gap-sync`. Running sync again may not help without recent ticket activity.
67	       * *Done ticket pre-dating first sync*: ticket is Done and was Done when the first full sync ran. See CLAUDE.md pitfall #27.
68	     - Offer: `(a) Create a manual mirror: bd create --title '<KEY> · <summary>' --label=src:jira --external-ref=<jira-url>; (b) Full investigation with /jira-show <KEY>.`
69	     - **Record any newly discovered failure mode** not matching the above: run `bd remember "jira-sync-failure: <concise description>"` before stopping.
70	     - Stop.
71	
72	3. **Run the bridge.** Execute `bd-timew start <resolved_id>` via a Bash tool call. Report the full stdout (it shows the issue, labels, and resolved `(client, case, svc)` tuple) and any stderr.
73	
74	4. **On failure** (non-zero exit), surface the error verbatim. Common causes:
75	   - No active beads workspace (`bd where` fails): tell the user to `cd` into a project that has `.beads/`.
76	   - Unknown bead id: check spelling with `bd list`.
77	   - `PyYAML` missing: the script's error message explains the fix.
78	
79	5. **Do not change bead status yourself.** `bd-timew start` already calls `bd update --claim` when appropriate. Don't double up.
80	
81	6. **Post-action:** no additional commentary unless the tuple looks wrong (e.g. `Svc: (none)` on what should be a billable bead) — in that case, flag it briefly and point at `.beads/bd-timew.yaml`.
82	
83	7. **Offer to scaffold a task directory.** Check whether `tasks/<resolved_id>/` exists; for `src:jira` beads, also check `tasks/<JIRA-KEY>/`. If either exists, skip this step silently. Otherwise, ask the user on one line: `Scaffold tasks/<id>/? [y/N]`. Do not try to judge appropriateness from the bead description — the user decides at the prompt. On `y`/`yes`, invoke `/task-init <resolved_id>` yourself (don't wait for the user to re-type); on anything else, skip and continue.
84	
85	8. **Proceed to implementation.** After the timer is running, any tuple concern is flagged, and the task-dir prompt is resolved, read the bead's description (already printed by step 3) and begin the work. The user invoked `/start` to start doing the issue — do not stop and ask for confirmation. Exception: if the bead is pure scoping/design with no concrete deliverable yet, say so in one line and prompt for direction instead of spinning up speculative work.

file.content:
---
name: start
description: Begin work on a Beads issue — claim it and start a Timewarrior interval tagged with its billing tuple
argument-hint: "<bead-id> | --jira <JIRA-KEY>"
author: Michael Haynes
scope: global
tags: [time-tracking, beads, timewarrior]
timestamps:
  - action: created
    at: 2026-04-20T00:35:06-05:00
    actor: Michael Haynes
  - action: updated
    at: 2026-04-21T11:30:00-05:00
    actor: Michael Haynes
    note: "Added --jira <KEY> as alternative to positional bead ID, with Jira-fallback resolution procedure"
comments:
  - "Source: J121-9kp.1 Wave 1 time-tracking bundle (2026-04-20). Companion to /stop, /switch, /status and the bd-timew bridge script at ~/.local/bin/bd-timew."
  - "Motivation: claiming a bead and starting time tracking were two separate rituals (bd update --claim + timew start) that were easy to skip. One command enforces the pairing."
  - "Projected use: invoke when starting work on a specific bead. Epic-level /start is OK for triage/refinement; /switch to a child bead as soon as concrete work begins so billing tuple resolves correctly."
  - "2026-04-21: Added --jira <JIRA-KEY> flag as an alternative to the positional bead ID. Resolves via local title search → Jira MCP verify → bd jira sync → retry; surfaces known pitfalls and offers manual-mirror fallback on continued failure. Positional ID and --jira are mutually exclusive."
related: [/stop, /switch, /status, /time-report]
---

# /start — Begin work on a Beads issue

Claim a Beads issue and start a Timewarrior interval tagged with its resolved billing tuple. Thin wrapper over `bd-timew start`.

Arguments: $ARGUMENTS

## Instructions

1. **Parse and validate arguments.** If `$ARGUMENTS` is empty or whitespace, print:
   ```
   /start: missing bead id
   usage: /start <bead-id>        (e.g. /start J121-abc)
          /start --jira <KEY>     (e.g. /start --jira BOCO-18077)
   ```
   and stop. Do **not** infer a bead from `bd ready`, in-progress lists, or context. Starting the timer on the wrong bead corrupts billing.

   Recognized forms:
   - Positional bead ID: first token that does not start with `-`
   - `--jira <KEY>`: flag + value (mutually exclusive with positional ID)
   - Both present simultaneously → usage error and stop.

2. **Resolve the bead ID.**
   - If a positional bead ID was given → `resolved_id = <id>`. Skip to step 3.
   - If `--jira <KEY>` → run the **Jira key resolution** procedure:

     **A. Local lookup.**
     ```bash
     bd list --label=src:jira --json 2>/dev/null | sed -n '1,/^]$/p' | \
       jq -r --arg k "KEY" '.[] | select(.title | test($k; "i")) | .id' | head -1
     ```
     If a bead ID is returned → `resolved_id = <that id>`. Proceed to step 3.

     **B. Jira verification** (if local lookup returned nothing). Call the Atlassian MCP tool `getJiraIssue` (cloudId `80b04637-628f-4df2-8bfa-012de201c08c`, issueIdOrKey `<KEY>`).
     - If MCP returns an error or 404: print `/start: ticket <KEY> not found in Jira or no access. Verify the key and Jira authentication.` and stop.
     - If ticket found → proceed to C.

     **C. Sync and retry.** Run `bd jira sync --pull` via Bash. Re-run the local lookup from A. If found → `resolved_id = <that id>`. Proceed to step 3.

     **D. Diagnose and surface** (if still not found after sync). Run `bd config show | grep pull_jql` to get the configured JQL. Check whether the Jira ticket's summary and assignee match it. Then report:
     - "Ticket `<KEY>` exists in Jira but could not be imported into Beads."
     - List applicable known reasons:
       * *JQL mismatch*: ticket isn't assigned to `currentUser()` or summary doesn't satisfy the filter — sync will never auto-import it. See CLAUDE.md pitfall #26.
       * *Incremental timing gap*: ticket matches the JQL but hasn't been updated since the last sync run. Known pitfall: `bd-jira-sync-pull-incremental-timing-gap-sync`. Running sync again may not help without recent ticket activity.
       * *Done ticket pre-dating first sync*: ticket is Done and was Done when the first full sync ran. See CLAUDE.md pitfall #27.
     - Offer: `(a) Create a manual mirror: bd create --title '<KEY> · <summary>' --label=src:jira --external-ref=<jira-url>; (b) Full investigation with /jira-show <KEY>.`
     - **Record any newly discovered failure mode** not matching the above: run `bd remember "jira-sync-failure: <concise description>"` before stopping.
     - Stop.

3. **Run the bridge.** Execute `bd-timew start <resolved_id>` via a Bash tool call. Report the full stdout (it shows the issue, labels, and resolved `(client, case, svc)` tuple) and any stderr.

4. **On failure** (non-zero exit), surface the error verbatim. Common causes:
   - No active beads workspace (`bd where` fails): tell the user to `cd` into a project that has `.beads/`.
   - Unknown bead id: check spelling with `bd list`.
   - `PyYAML` missing: the script's error message explains the fix.

5. **Do not change bead status yourself.** `bd-timew start` already calls `bd update --claim` when appropriate. Don't double up.

6. **Post-action:** no additional commentary unless the tuple looks wrong (e.g. `Svc: (none)` on what should be a billable bead) — in that case, flag it briefly and point at `.beads/bd-timew.yaml`.

7. **Offer to scaffold a task directory.** Check whether `tasks/<resolved_id>/` exists; for `src:jira` beads, also check `tasks/<JIRA-KEY>/`. If either exists, skip this step silently. Otherwise, ask the user on one line: `Scaffold tasks/<id>/? [y/N]`. Do not try to judge appropriateness from the bead description — the user decides at the prompt. On `y`/`yes`, invoke `/task-init <resolved_id>` yourself (don't wait for the user to re-type); on anything else, skip and continue.

8. **Proceed to implementation.** After the timer is running, any tuple concern is flagged, and the task-dir prompt is resolved, read the bead's description (already printed by step 3) and begin the work. The user invoked `/start` to start doing the issue — do not stop and ask for confirmation. Exception: if the bead is pure scoping/design with no concrete deliverable yet, say so in one line and prompt for direction instead of spinning up speculative work.

## Related

- `/stop`, `/switch`, `/status` — the rest of the time-tracking family.
- The `time-tracking` skill has the full ritual, tuple resolution order, and reporting pointers.
